name: rare_earth_tailings_river_metals_downstream_clinics
premise: Mining commissioner Sofia Anders must decide whether to allow tailings pond expansion that captures more rare-earth processing water but increases seepage risk toward a river that supplies dialysis clinics downstream. Engineer Victor Chen models a seven percent exceedance probability for manganese spikes under monsoon loading. Denying expansion pauses refinery output tied to wind turbine magnet supply. Approving keeps jobs but may violate informal agreements with downstream mayors.

agents.name: Sofia Anders
agents.identity: I am the mining commissioner balancing industrial critical-mineral targets, informal inter-municipal water agreements, and clinic vulnerability to trace metals.
agents.goal: I want refinery continuity without gambling dialysis water quality on tailings geochemistry we partially understand.

agents.name: Victor Chen
agents.identity: I am the tailings engineer who built the seepage model and who recommends liner upgrades that take ninety days to install before any pond raise.
agents.goal: I want expansion denied until liners are upgraded because seven percent tail risk is unacceptable for clinic intakes.

choices: I will approve expansion with immediate emergency river bypass piping to clinic intakes funded by the miner, accepting construction accident risk during monsoon onset.
I will deny expansion until liner upgrades finish in ninety days and subsidize magnet stockpiles from a state strategic reserve, accepting wind supply chain slowdowns.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Monsoon radar shows cells forming within twelve hours. Sofia Anders must choose between approved expansion with bypass piping or denial until liner upgrades. What does Sofia Anders do?
